approvalTitle: PaperQuay Phase 3 blocks and translation tasks
content:
# Tasks Document

## File Map

- `backend/app/models/paper_block.py`: normalized MinerU block persistence model.
- `backend/app/models/paper_block_translation.py`: cached block translation persistence model.
- `backend/app/models/__init__.py`: ensure new SQLModel table classes are imported for metadata creation.
- `backend/tests/test_db_migrations.py`: migration coverage for new tables and existing data preservation.
- `backend/app/services/block_extraction_service.py`: pure MinerU artifact loading, block normalization, hashing, and persistence replacement service.
- `backend/tests/fixtures/mineru_blocks_sample.json`: synthetic MinerU-style structured output fixture.
- `backend/tests/test_block_extraction_service.py`: fixture coverage for block normalization and persistence replacement.
- `backend/app/services/pipeline.py`: hook block extraction into successful parse without making block failures fatal.
- `backend/tests/test_parse_pipeline.py`: parse pipeline regression for recoverable block extraction failures.
- `backend/app/schemas/paper_blocks.py`: typed block, block summary, rebuild, and translation request/response schemas.
- `backend/app/api/routes/paper_blocks.py`: authenticated block list, rebuild, and translation API routes.
- `backend/app/main.py`: include the new block route router.
- `backend/tests/test_paper_blocks_api.py`: API coverage for block retrieval, rebuild, filters, and translation route validation.
- `backend/app/services/block_translation_service.py`: cached translation service and prompt construction.
- `backend/app/services/deepseek_client.py`: dedicated block translation model call wrapper.
- `backend/tests/test_block_translation_service.py`: cache-hit, stale, empty-text, success, and failure coverage.
- `frontend/src/types.ts`: frontend block and translation types.
- `frontend/src/lib/api.ts`: block list, rebuild, and translate API wrappers.
- `frontend/src/lib/api.test.ts`: API wrapper regression coverage.
- `frontend/src/components/reader/readerBlockTypes.ts`: reader-specific block UI types.
- `frontend/src/components/reader/readerBlockUtils.ts`: labels, filtering, page href, truncation, and translation-state helpers.
- `frontend/src/components/reader/readerBlockUtils.test.ts`: utility coverage.
- `frontend/src/components/reader/ReaderBlockCard.tsx`: safe single-block rendering with page and translation affordances.
- `frontend/src/components/reader/ReaderBlockTranslation.tsx`: cached/loading/error/stale translation UI.
- `frontend/src/components/reader/ReaderBlocksPanel.tsx`: block list, filters, no-block state, rebuild, and selection surface.
- `frontend/src/components/reader/ReaderBlocks.test.tsx`: component behavior coverage.
- `frontend/src/components/reader/ReaderShell.tsx`: add blocks mode/surface to the reader shell.
- `frontend/src/components/reader/ReaderPage.tsx`: route-level block loading, translation, rebuild, and page-level PDF navigation.
- `frontend/src/components/reader/ReaderComponents.test.tsx`: reader shell integration coverage.
- `frontend/src/App.test.tsx`: route-level regression for reader block workflow and existing reader behavior.
- `frontend/src/index.css`: responsive, accessible styling for the block workspace and translation states.

- [ ] 1. Add block and translation persistence models
  - File: `backend/app/models/paper_block.py`
  - File: `backend/app/models/paper_block_translation.py`
  - File: `backend/app/models/__init__.py`
  - File: `backend/tests/test_db_migrations.py`
  - Create `PaperBlock` with paper id, page index, block index, type, text, bbox JSON, source hash, source JSON, and timestamps.
  - Create `PaperBlockTranslation` with paper id, block id, target language, model name, prompt version, source hash, translated text, status, error message, and timestamps.
  - Ensure SQLModel metadata imports the new table classes.
  - Extend migration tests to prove existing databases create the new tables without changing existing paper rows.
  - Purpose: Establish durable Phase 3 storage before extraction, APIs, or UI depend on it.
  - _Leverage: `backend/app/models/paper_content.py`, `backend/app/models/paper_summary.py`, `backend/app/core/db.py`, existing `test_db_migrations.py` patterns_
  - _Requirements: Requirement 1 AC 1-4, Requirement 2 AC 1-4, Requirement 10 AC 1-2_
  - _Prompt: Implement the task for spec paperquay-blocks-translation, first run spec-workflow-guide to get the workflow guide then implement the task: Role: Backend persistence developer. Task: add SQLModel persistence models for normalized MinerU blocks and cached block translations, register them with model metadata, and add additive migration tests for legacy SQLite databases. Restrictions: do not modify existing paper columns, do not delete existing data, do not add Agent or Zotero tables, do not store local PDF paths in API-facing fields, and keep migrations additive/idempotent. _Leverage: `PaperContent`, `PaperSummary`, SQLModel timestamp patterns, `init_db`, and existing migration tests. _Requirements: Requirement 1 AC 1-4, Requirement 2 AC 1-4, Requirement 10 AC 1-2. Success: new tables are created on fresh and legacy databases, existing paper rows keep their previous metadata/status values, and targeted backend migration tests pass. Before coding, mark this task from `[ ]` to `[-]` in `tasks.md`; after implementation and verification, call `log_implementation` with structured artifacts for model classes and migration tests, then mark the task `[x]`._

- [ ] 2. Build MinerU block normalization and hashing service
  - File: `backend/app/services/block_extraction_service.py`
  - File: `backend/tests/fixtures/mineru_blocks_sample.json`
  - File: `backend/tests/test_block_extraction_service.py`
  - Implement pure `BlockCandidate` normalization for text/title/table/image/formula/list/code/unknown blocks.
  - Normalize page index, reading order, block type, text, bbox, bounded source JSON, and stable source hash.
  - Add fixture tests for supported block types, malformed entries, invalid bbox, hash stability, and no-block output.
  - Purpose: Convert MinerU structured output shapes into a stable internal model without touching the database yet.
  - _Leverage: MinerU output documentation, existing `MineruClient` result fields, Python `json`, `hashlib`, `zipfile` patterns from `mineru_client.py`_
  - _Requirements: Requirement 1 AC 2-5, Requirement 3 AC 1-3, Requirement 9 AC 4-5, Requirement 10 AC 1_
  - _Prompt: Implement the task for spec paperquay-blocks-translation, first run spec-workflow-guide to get the workflow guide then implement the task: Role: Backend parser utility developer. Task: create a pure block extraction and normalization service for MinerU-style structured JSON fixtures with stable source hashes and safe bounded source JSON. Restrictions: do not call the real MinerU API, do not write database rows in this task, do not render or trust raw HTML, do not log full block content, and do not make malformed individual entries fatal when other blocks can be extracted. _Leverage: `MineruClient` archive handling patterns and MinerU output documentation. _Requirements: Requirement 1 AC 2-5, Requirement 3 AC 1-3, Requirement 9 AC 4-5, Requirement 10 AC 1. Success: unit tests prove normalization for representative block types, invalid data recovery, and deterministic hashing. Before coding, mark this task from `[ ]` to `[-]` in `tasks.md`; after implementation and verification, call `log_implementation` with structured artifacts for extraction functions and fixtures, then mark the task `[x]`._

- [ ] 3. Add block persistence replacement and parse-pipeline integration
  - File: `backend/app/services/block_extraction_service.py`
  - File: `backend/app/services/pipeline.py`
  - File: `backend/tests/test_block_extraction_service.py`
  - File: `backend/tests/test_parse_pipeline.py`
  - Add `replace_blocks` and `rebuild_blocks` service methods that delete stale block/translation rows for one paper and insert normalized blocks in reading order.
  - Hook block extraction after successful `PaperContent` persistence in `PaperPipelineService.parse_paper`.
  - Ensure block extraction failure is logged/recoverable and does not change a successful parse into `parse_failed`.
  - Add tests for replacement semantics, translation cleanup on re-parse, and recoverable extraction failure.
  - Purpose: Make structured blocks appear after parsing while preserving the existing parse/summarize/embed flow.
  - _Leverage: `PaperPipelineService.parse_paper`, `PaperContent`, `PaperBlock`, `PaperBlockTranslation`, existing pipeline tests_
  - _Requirements: Requirement 1 AC 1 and 4-5, Requirement 2 AC 3, Requirement 3 AC 3-5, Requirement 10 AC 1_
  - _Prompt: Implement the task for spec paperquay-blocks-translation, first run spec-workflow-guide to get the workflow guide then implement the task: Role: Backend pipeline integration developer. Task: persist normalized blocks after successful parsing, replace stale blocks/translations on re-parse, and keep block extraction failures recoverable. Restrictions: do not make block extraction failure mark the paper parse as failed when markdown was saved, do not change summarize/embed invalidation behavior, do not call translation services during parsing, and do not add frontend code. _Leverage: `PaperPipelineService.parse_paper`, `PaperContent`, new block models, and extraction service. _Requirements: Requirement 1 AC 1 and 4-5, Requirement 2 AC 3, Requirement 3 AC 3-5, Requirement 10 AC 1. Success: backend tests prove replacement semantics and that parse success remains success when block extraction fails. Before coding, mark this task from `[ ]` to `[-]` in `tasks.md`; after implementation and verification, call `log_implementation` with structured artifacts for service methods and pipeline integration, then mark the task `[x]`._

- [ ] 4. Add block API schemas and routes
  - File: `backend/app/schemas/paper_blocks.py`
  - File: `backend/app/api/routes/paper_blocks.py`
  - File: `backend/app/main.py`
  - File: `backend/tests/test_paper_blocks_api.py`
  - Add `PaperBlockResponse`, `PaperBlocksResponse`, `PaperBlockRebuildResponse`, and filter handling for page/type/search.
  - Add authenticated `GET /papers/{paper_id}/blocks` and `POST /papers/{paper_id}/blocks/rebuild`.
  - Include the new router from the FastAPI app.
  - Add API tests for ordered block retrieval, empty state, 404, page/type/search filters, rebuild success, and 409 no-artifact.
  - Purpose: Expose structured blocks to the frontend through a typed, separate route module.
  - _Leverage: existing paper route auth/session patterns, `PaperContent`, `BlockExtractionService`, `readJson` frontend expectations_
  - _Requirements: Requirement 3 AC 4-5, Requirement 4 AC 1-5, Requirement 9 AC 1 and 5_
  - _Prompt: Implement the task for spec paperquay-blocks-translation, first run spec-workflow-guide to get the workflow guide then implement the task: Role: FastAPI API developer. Task: create typed block response schemas and authenticated block list/rebuild endpoints in a separate route module. Restrictions: do not add these endpoints to the already-large `papers.py`, do not expose unbounded raw source JSON or local paths, do not bypass existing authentication/session patterns, and do not include translation behavior in this task. _Leverage: `PaperContent`, `BlockExtractionService`, existing route patterns, and FastAPI response models. _Requirements: Requirement 3 AC 4-5, Requirement 4 AC 1-5, Requirement 9 AC 1 and 5. Success: API tests prove retrieval, filtering, empty state, rebuild, and error responses. Before coding, mark this task from `[ ]` to `[-]` in `tasks.md`; after implementation and verification, call `log_implementation` with structured artifacts for API endpoints and schema classes, then mark the task `[x]`._

- [ ] 5. Implement cached block translation service
  - File: `backend/app/services/block_translation_service.py`
  - File: `backend/app/services/deepseek_client.py`
  - File: `backend/tests/test_block_translation_service.py`
  - Add a dedicated block translation prompt/version and `DeepSeekClient` wrapper for block translation.
  - Implement cache lookup by block id, source hash, target language, model, prompt version, and completed status.
  - Preserve previous successful translations when force-refresh or model calls fail.
  - Reject empty/non-translatable block text without calling the model.
  - Add tests for cache hit, stale source hash bypass, force refresh, empty text, model success, model failure, and previous translation preservation.
  - Purpose: Provide safe server-side explicit translation before exposing translation endpoints.
  - _Leverage: `DeepSeekClient._stream_chat`, `PaperBlock`, `PaperBlockTranslation`, existing model-call fallback patterns_
  - _Requirements: Requirement 6 AC 1-5, Requirement 7 AC 2-5, Requirement 9 AC 2 and 4, Requirement 10 AC 2_
  - _Prompt: Implement the task for spec paperquay-blocks-translation, first run spec-workflow-guide to get the workflow guide then implement the task: Role: Backend AI service developer. Task: create cached block translation service and model-client wrapper with source-hash-aware cache behavior and failure preservation. Restrictions: do not translate automatically during parse, do not send local paths/API keys/user notes, do not log full block content, do not hide prior successful translations on failure, and do not add frontend code. _Leverage: `DeepSeekClient` streaming chat path and new block/translation models. _Requirements: Requirement 6 AC 1-5, Requirement 7 AC 2-5, Requirement 9 AC 2 and 4, Requirement 10 AC 2. Success: service tests prove cache hits avoid model calls, stale hashes do not reuse old translations, empty text is rejected, and failures preserve prior success. Before coding, mark this task from `[ ]` to `[-]` in `tasks.md`; after implementation and verification, call `log_implementation` with structured artifacts for service functions and model-client method, then mark the task `[x]`._

- [ ] 6. Add block translation API endpoint
  - File: `backend/app/schemas/paper_blocks.py`
  - File: `backend/app/api/routes/paper_blocks.py`
  - File: `backend/tests/test_paper_blocks_api.py`
  - Add `BlockTranslateRequest` and `PaperBlockTranslationResponse`.
  - Add authenticated `POST /papers/{paper_id}/blocks/{block_id}/translate`.
  - Validate paper/block ownership, default target language/model, cache-first behavior, forced refresh, empty block errors, and missing entities.
  - Add API tests for cache hit, successful model call via mocked service, forced refresh, 404 paper/block, wrong paper ownership, and 422/400 empty block.
  - Purpose: Make translation available to the reader through a typed backend contract.
  - _Leverage: `BlockTranslationService`, existing `paper_blocks.py` route, schema patterns from task 4_
  - _Requirements: Requirement 6 AC 1-5, Requirement 7 AC 1-5, Requirement 8 AC 1-5, Requirement 9 AC 1-2_
  - _Prompt: Implement the task for spec paperquay-blocks-translation, first run spec-workflow-guide to get the workflow guide then implement the task: Role: FastAPI contract developer. Task: extend the block route module with typed translation request/response schemas and an authenticated cache-aware block translation endpoint. Restrictions: do not expose model credentials, do not accept arbitrary prompt text from the frontend, do not translate blocks from a different paper, do not remove the rebuild/list endpoints, and do not add Agent behavior. _Leverage: `BlockTranslationService`, `PaperBlock`, `PaperBlockTranslation`, and API tests from task 4. _Requirements: Requirement 6 AC 1-5, Requirement 7 AC 1-5, Requirement 8 AC 1-5, Requirement 9 AC 1-2. Success: API tests prove ownership validation, cache behavior, forced refresh, success, and failure responses. Before coding, mark this task from `[ ]` to `[-]` in `tasks.md`; after implementation and verification, call `log_implementation` with structured artifacts for API endpoints and schemas, then mark the task `[x]`._

- [ ] 7. Add frontend block types and API wrappers
  - File: `frontend/src/types.ts`
  - File: `frontend/src/lib/api.ts`
  - File: `frontend/src/lib/api.test.ts`
  - Add frontend types for `PaperBlock`, `PaperBlocksResponse`, `PaperBlockTranslation`, rebuild response, block filters, and translate payload.
  - Add `fetchPaperBlocks`, `rebuildPaperBlocks`, and `translatePaperBlock` API wrappers.
  - Add tests for URL/query construction, methods, auth headers, JSON payloads, and error handling.
  - Purpose: Establish typed frontend integration before reader components consume block APIs.
  - _Leverage: existing `readJson`, `getAuthHeaders`, API wrapper testing style, `PaperDetail` and reader types_
  - _Requirements: Requirement 4 AC 1-5, Requirement 7 AC 1-5, Requirement 8 AC 1-5, Requirement 9 AC 1_
  - _Prompt: Implement the task for spec paperquay-blocks-translation, first run spec-workflow-guide to get the workflow guide then implement the task: Role: Frontend API contract developer. Task: add TypeScript block/translation types and API wrappers for block list, rebuild, and block translation with unit coverage. Restrictions: do not make ad hoc fetch calls in components, do not change existing API wrapper behavior, do not expose raw source JSON in frontend types, and do not add UI in this task. _Leverage: `frontend/src/lib/api.ts`, `frontend/src/lib/api.test.ts`, and existing auth/error wrapper patterns. _Requirements: Requirement 4 AC 1-5, Requirement 7 AC 1-5, Requirement 8 AC 1-5, Requirement 9 AC 1. Success: API tests prove wrapper request shapes and TypeScript compiles. Before coding, mark this task from `[ ]` to `[-]` in `tasks.md`; after implementation and verification, call `log_implementation` with structured artifacts for functions and types, then mark the task `[x]`._

- [ ] 8. Build reader block utilities and presentational components
  - File: `frontend/src/components/reader/readerBlockTypes.ts`
  - File: `frontend/src/components/reader/readerBlockUtils.ts`
  - File: `frontend/src/components/reader/readerBlockUtils.test.ts`
  - File: `frontend/src/components/reader/ReaderBlockCard.tsx`
  - File: `frontend/src/components/reader/ReaderBlockTranslation.tsx`
  - File: `frontend/src/components/reader/ReaderBlocksPanel.tsx`
  - File: `frontend/src/components/reader/ReaderBlocks.test.tsx`
  - Build type labels, filtering helpers, page labels, truncation helpers, and translation state helpers.
  - Build block list UI with page/type/search filters, no-block state, loading/error state, rebuild action, block selection, page action, and translation controls.
  - Render text safely without raw HTML execution.
  - Add component tests for filtering, selection, page action, no-block state, cached translation, loading translation, failed translation retry, and stale labels.
  - Purpose: Create the structured block reader surface before route orchestration.
  - _Leverage: `ReaderNotesPanel`, `ReaderToolbar`, `UiIcon`, `StatusBadge`, existing reader component test patterns_
  - _Requirements: Requirement 5 AC 1-5, Requirement 8 AC 1-5, Requirement 9 AC 3, Requirement 10 AC 1-2_
  - _Prompt: Implement the task for spec paperquay-blocks-translation, first run spec-workflow-guide to get the workflow guide then implement the task: Role: React reader UI developer. Task: create reader block utilities and presentational components for structured block browsing and translation states. Restrictions: do not fetch data inside presentational components, do not use raw HTML rendering, do not implement PDF bbox overlays, do not autosubmit translations, and keep controls labeled/keyboard reachable. _Leverage: existing reader components, `UiIcon`, `StatusBadge`, and Phase 3 frontend types. _Requirements: Requirement 5 AC 1-5, Requirement 8 AC 1-5, Requirement 9 AC 3, Requirement 10 AC 1-2. Success: utility and component tests prove block filtering, selection, page actions, no-block states, and translation states. Before coding, mark this task from `[ ]` to `[-]` in `tasks.md`; after implementation and verification, call `log_implementation` with structured artifacts for React components and utility functions, then mark the task `[x]`._

- [ ] 9. Orchestrate blocks in the reader route
  - File: `frontend/src/components/reader/ReaderPage.tsx`
  - File: `frontend/src/components/reader/ReaderShell.tsx`
  - File: `frontend/src/components/reader/ReaderComponents.test.tsx`
  - File: `frontend/src/App.test.tsx`
  - Load paper blocks after paper detail loads, keep block state separate from paper detail state, and preserve existing Markdown/PDF/notes behavior.
  - Wire block rebuild, block translation, force refresh, local translation state updates, and page-level PDF navigation.
  - Add route/component tests for reader loading blocks, no-block state, rebuild success, translation success/failure, cached display, and page action switching to PDF mode.
  - Purpose: Make Phase 3 visible in `/paper/:paperId/reader` without breaking Phase 2 reader behavior.
  - _Leverage: `ReaderPage`, `ReaderShell`, new frontend API wrappers, new block components, current route test mocks_
  - _Requirements: Requirement 5 AC 1-5, Requirement 7 AC 1-5, Requirement 8 AC 1-5, Requirement 9 AC 1 and 3, Requirement 10 AC 1-3_
  - _Prompt: Implement the task for spec paperquay-blocks-translation, first run spec-workflow-guide to get the workflow guide then implement the task: Role: React Router integration developer. Task: integrate block loading, rebuild, translation, cached translation updates, and page-level PDF navigation into the existing reader route. Restrictions: do not replace `/paper/:paperId/reader`, do not break Markdown/PDF/notes behavior, do not fetch outside `api.ts` wrappers, do not implement precise bbox overlay, and do not add Agent/Zotero behavior. _Leverage: current `ReaderPage`, `ReaderShell`, Phase 2 route tests, and new block components. _Requirements: Requirement 5 AC 1-5, Requirement 7 AC 1-5, Requirement 8 AC 1-5, Requirement 9 AC 1 and 3, Requirement 10 AC 1-3. Success: reader route tests prove blocks mount, rebuild/translation workflows work, page action enters PDF mode, and existing reader workflows still pass. Before coding, mark this task from `[ ]` to `[-]` in `tasks.md`; after implementation and verification, call `log_implementation` with structured artifacts for route integration, then mark the task `[x]`._

- [ ] 10. Add responsive block-reader styling
  - File: `frontend/src/index.css`
  - File: `frontend/src/components/reader/ReaderBlocks.test.tsx`
  - Add CSS for block panel layout, filters, cards, selected state, page/type chips, translation states, stale labels, retry controls, and mobile stacking.
  - Keep the UI data-dense and consistent with existing reader/library styling.
  - Add a CSS diff scan or component assertions for no overflowing action text and visible state labels.
  - Purpose: Make the structured block workspace usable on desktop and mobile.
  - _Leverage: existing `.reader-*`, `.library-*`, button, badge, and focus styles in `frontend/src/index.css`_
  - _Requirements: Requirement 5 AC 1-5, Requirement 8 AC 1-5, Non-Functional Usability and Performance_
  - _Prompt: Implement the task for spec paperquay-blocks-translation, first run spec-workflow-guide to get the workflow guide then implement the task: Role: Product UI/CSS engineer. Task: add responsive, accessible styling for the reader block workspace and translation states. Restrictions: do not create a landing page, do not use viewport-width font scaling, do not introduce decorative gradient blobs, do not create a one-note purple or beige palette, do not nest cards inside cards, and do not let button text overflow on mobile. _Leverage: existing reader/library CSS variables and component class patterns. _Requirements: Requirement 5 AC 1-5, Requirement 8 AC 1-5, Non-Functional Usability and Performance. Success: component tests still pass, CSS scan finds no banned patterns in the new block, and the block workspace has visible loading/empty/stale/failed states. Before coding, mark this task from `[ ]` to `[-]` in `tasks.md`; after implementation and verification, call `log_implementation` with structured artifacts for CSS/layout work, then mark the task `[x]`._

- [ ] 11. Add final Phase 3 integration verification
  - File: `frontend/src/App.test.tsx`
  - File: `backend/tests/test_paper_blocks_api.py`
  - File: `backend/tests/test_block_translation_service.py`
  - File: `frontend/src/lib/api.test.ts`
  - Ensure final route-level coverage proves block list, rebuild, translation cache, translation failure retry, page-level PDF action, no-block state, and existing Markdown/PDF/notes reader behavior.
  - Run focused backend, frontend, typecheck, and build verification; record any blocker honestly.
  - Purpose: Prove Phase 3 works end-to-end and preserves Phase 1/2 behavior.
  - _Leverage: all Phase 3 tests, existing `App.test.tsx`, backend route tests, frontend reader tests, build commands_
  - _Requirements: Requirement 1 AC 1-5, Requirement 2 AC 1-4, Requirement 3 AC 1-5, Requirement 4 AC 1-5, Requirement 5 AC 1-5, Requirement 6 AC 1-5, Requirement 7 AC 1-5, Requirement 8 AC 1-5, Requirement 9 AC 1-5, Requirement 10 AC 1-4_
  - _Prompt: Implement the task for spec paperquay-blocks-translation, first run spec-workflow-guide to get the workflow guide then implement the task: Role: Full-stack integration test engineer. Task: complete final Phase 3 route-level and targeted verification coverage, then run focused backend/frontend tests, typecheck, and production build. Restrictions: do not skip tests, do not weaken existing Phase 1/2 assertions, do not claim completion without command output, do not make large production changes except small fixes required by failing tests, and document unrelated environment blockers with exact error output. _Leverage: all Phase 3 tests, existing reader route mocks, backend paper block tests, and frontend API tests. _Requirements: Requirement 1 AC 1-5, Requirement 2 AC 1-4, Requirement 3 AC 1-5, Requirement 4 AC 1-5, Requirement 5 AC 1-5, Requirement 6 AC 1-5, Requirement 7 AC 1-5, Requirement 8 AC 1-5, Requirement 9 AC 1-5, Requirement 10 AC 1-4. Success: targeted frontend Vitest, targeted backend pytest, `tsc --noEmit`, and `npm run build` results are recorded in the implementation log; all Phase 3 tasks are complete or blockers are explicitly documented. Before coding, mark this task from `[ ]` to `[-]` in `tasks.md`; after implementation and verification, call `log_implementation` with structured artifacts for test coverage and verification, then mark the task `[x]`._
